mechanism_narrative: Rising CPIAUCSL levels increase inflation expectations, prompting equity investors to demand higher risk premiums, which mechanically depresses S&P 500 forward returns over a one-month horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC exceeds 0.05 or positive 20-day returns occur in >65% of high-CPIAUCSL observations over a 3-year walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When UNRATE crosses above 4.0%, labor market slack signals imminent corporate earnings deterioration, causing immediate negative repricing in S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: Forward 5-day returns remain positive in >55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 4.0% during backtesting.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in RSAFS and HOUST indicates robust consumer and housing demand, which sustains corporate revenue growth and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over a quarterly horizon.
failure_condition: Joint positive signals yield negative 60-day returns in >60% of cases, or interaction term loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in quarterly regression models.

mechanism_narrative: Declining INDPRO levels during a monetary tightening cycle reflect constrained production capacity that outweighs rate hike benefits, generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening periods produce positive 20-day returns in >58% of observations, invalidating the supply-constraint premium.

mechanism_narrative: Higher GDPC1 values reflect stronger aggregate economic output, which directly boosts corporate profit expectations and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: Rank IC falls below 0.02 over rolling 2-year windows, or 5-day forward returns show no statistical difference from zero when GDPC1 is above its 10-year median.

mechanism_narrative: When FEDFUNDS drops below 2.0%, ultra-loose monetary policy reduces discount rates and stimulates risk appetite, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over a three-month horizon.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in >60% of periods where FEDFUNDS remains below 2.0%, or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for inflation expectations.

mechanism_narrative: Rising PAYEMS combined with falling UNRATE indicates a tightening labor market that increases wage pressures and compresses corporate margins, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over one month.
failure_condition: The joint labor tightening signal yields positive 20-day returns in >55% of cases, or the interaction effect becomes statistically insignificant in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Sharp declines in INDPRO during a post-shock recovery phase signal persistent supply chain bottlenecks that delay earnings rebounds, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next trading week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns remain positive in >60% of post-shock recovery periods following a 1%+ INDPRO decline, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark.
